Transvestitism as a repeated practice (COM.practice) strongly resonates with material aspects (clothing), personal experience, aesthetic value, and transformative symmetry (identity change). Ethical dimensions arise from societal judgments, while its generative and independent nature aligns with skill acquisition through rehearsal.

Unwritten laws in the context of practice resonate most strongly with communal/societal scale (scale.community/society), relational ontology (ontological.relational), essential resonance (resonance.essential), and ethical value (value.ethical). They reflect learned norms reinforced through repeated practice (symmetry.cyclical), experienced through comprehension (experience.comprehension), and predictably influencing behavior (lawfulness.predictable).

Usage in the context of COM.practice strongly resonates with continuity (ongoing repetition), individual scale, experiential learning, structural patterns, independence from external factors, essential core of skill acquisition, predictable routines, comprehension through practice, and functional value in skill development.

Usance resonates most with temporal continuity, establishment, and transformation as a repeated practice. It operates at individual/community scales, generates skill (functional value), and aligns with cyclical/generative symmetry. Its essence ties to experiential learning and structural patterns in practice.

The highest scores reflect practice's repetitive nature (cyclical), its transformative impact on skill (transformative), its ongoing persistence (continuation), and its practical utility (functional). Lower scores align with its individual focus and lack of broader systemic or existential resonance.

The highest scores reflect practice's role in transforming skill (transformative symmetry), its essential core in skill acquisition (resonance.essential), and its functional utility in improving ability. Practice also manifests as an ongoing, structural process (temporal.continuation, ontological.structural) and deepens experiential understanding (epistemological.experience).

The word 'vex' most strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (frustration as a necessary part of practice), catalytic interconnection (frustration driving further practice), and essential resonance (touching the core struggle in mastery). It also aligns with the continuation of practice over time and experiential understanding gained through persistent effort.

Victimise resonates with destructive symmetry (5), ethical value (4), and relational dynamics (3) in practice contexts. Repeated harmful actions (interconnection.chained, 3) and their temporal influence (3) reflect systemic harm. Lower scores reflect its limited alignment with COM.practice's core definition of skill acquisition.

Victimization resonates most with ethical implications (value_generation.ethical), relational power dynamics (ontological.relational), and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). It weakly connects to temporal effects (transformation/influence) and cause-effect chains (interconnection.chained).

The phrase 'walk through' in practice contexts emphasizes iterative, experiential skill-building (epistemological.experience 7, value.functional 7). It resonates with relational skill development (ontological.relational 5) and cyclical repetition (symmetry.generative 5, symmetry.transformative 4). Scores decline for aspects tied to teaching (symmetric pair) or grander scales/resonances.

Walk-through resonates most with structural patterns (resonance.structural), predictable repetition (lawfulness.predictable), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It also involves embodied experience (epistemological.experience) and superficial simplicity (resonance.superficial).

Witching as practice emphasizes individual skill-building (scale.individual, 7) and transformative mastery (symmetry.transformative, 5). It involves experiential learning (epistemological.experience, 5) and structural repetition (ontological.structural, 3). The sacred layer (value.sacred, 5) reflects its ritualistic depth, while aesthetic resonance (value.aesthetic, 3) ties to ritual beauty. Temporal.continuation (3) and cyclical (symmetry.cyclical, 2) scores reflect ongoing practice patterns.
